name: Atheris (Google)
pricing_notes: Fully open source (Apache 2.0); no hosted service

performance.notes: Coverage-guided fuzzer; runs indefinitely finding new paths; iteration speed depends on code under test

agent_readiness.notes: Google's Python fuzzing library using libFuzzer. Coverage-guided fuzzing for Python and Python-wrapped C/C++ extensions. Finds crashes, exceptions, and undefined behavior. Simple decorator-based API (just add @atheris.instrument_func). Integrates with OSS-Fuzz for continuous fuzzing. Used internally at Google.

gotchas: Best for finding crashes/panics — not semantic/logic bugs
Requires manual harness writing (define input corpus and test function)
best_when: Security-critical Python code handling untrusted input (parsers, serializers)
Python C extension libraries needing crash detection
Libraries integrated with OSS-Fuzz for continuous fuzzing
Finding edge cases in input parsing code
avoid_when: High-level application testing (not designed for end-to-end)
summary: Atheris is Google's coverage-guided Python fuzzing library using libFuzzer. Finds crashes, exceptions, and undefined behavior in Python code and Python-wrapped C extensions. Simple decorator API. Integrates with OSS-Fuzz for continuous cloud fuzzing. Linux-primary. Best for security-critical Python code handling untrusted input (parsers, protocol implementations, format converters).
